description: Bring logging across the platform to the highest standard: consistent prefixes, timestamps, contextual error logging, and debug-level request tracing.

userStories.title: Standardize browser extension log tag prefix to [opentabs]
userStories.description: As a developer debugging the browser extension, I want all console log messages to use a consistent [opentabs] prefix so that I can reliably filter logs in Chrome DevTools.
acceptanceCriteria: Every console.warn/console.log/console.error in platform/browser-extension/src/ uses either [opentabs] or [opentabs:<context>] prefix
No [OpenTabs] (capital O, capital T) prefixes exist anywhere in browser-extension/src/
The console.warn injected into target tabs in tool-dispatch.ts uses [opentabs] prefix
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/browser-extension/src/messaging.ts: 2 places — line 10 '[OpenTabs] sendToServer failed' and line 18 '[OpenTabs] forwardToSidePanel failed'. Change [OpenTabs] to [opentabs].
- platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.ts: 2 places in IIFE-injected teardown code — line 215 and line 329 both have console.warn('[OpenTabs] teardown error:', e). Change [OpenTabs] to [opentabs].
- platform/browser-extension/src/tool-dispatch.ts: line 35 — console.warn(`[OpenTabs] ${pName}.${tName} invoked`). Change [OpenTabs] to [opentabs].
- platform/browser-extension/src/plugin-storage.ts: line 102 — console.warn(`[plugin-storage] Skipping corrupted...`). Change [plugin-storage] to [opentabs].

Do NOT change [opentabs:background] or [opentabs:offscreen] — those already follow the colon-delimited context pattern.

userStories.title: Replace bare .catch(console.error) with contextual error handlers in background.ts
userStories.description: As a developer debugging the browser extension, I want every catch handler in background.ts to include a descriptive prefix so that I can immediately identify which operation failed.
acceptanceCriteria: Each catch handler uses console.warn with [opentabs] prefix and operation name
userStories.notes:
In background.ts, replace each .catch(console.error) with a contextual handler:
- Line 83: injectPluginsIntoTab → .catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] tab injection failed:', err))
- Line 85: checkTabStateChanges → .catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] tab state check failed:', err))
- Line 90: checkTabStateChanges (onRemoved) → .catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] tab state check failed:', err))
- Line 132: sendTabSyncAll → .catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] tab sync failed:', err))
- Line 175: ensureOffscreenDocument (alarm) → .catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] offscreen creation failed:', err))
- Line 194: ensureOffscreenDocument (top-level) → .catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] offscreen creation failed:', err))
- Line 195: setupKeepaliveAlarm → .catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] keepalive alarm failed:', err))
- Line 196: reinjectStoredPlugins → .catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] plugin reinjection failed:', err))

Use console.warn not console.error — these are expected lifecycle failures (tabs closing, offscreen racing, etc.).

userStories.title: Replace bare .catch(console.error) with contextual error handlers in message-router.ts
userStories.description: As a developer debugging the browser extension, I want every catch handler in message-router.ts to include a descriptive prefix so that I can immediately identify which handler failed.
acceptanceCriteria: Each catch handler uses console.warn with [opentabs] prefix and the JSON-RPC method name
userStories.notes:
In message-router.ts, replace each .catch(console.error) with a contextual handler. The pattern is:
  handler(params, id).catch(console.error)
becomes:
  handler(params, id).catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] <method> handler failed:', err))

Use the JSON-RPC method name for <method>:
- handleSyncFull → 'sync.full'
- handlePluginUpdate → 'plugin.update'
- handlePluginUninstall → 'plugin.uninstall'
- handleToolDispatch → 'tool.dispatch'
- handleBrowserListTabs → 'browser.listTabs'
- handleBrowserOpenTab → 'browser.openTab'
- handleBrowserCloseTab → 'browser.closeTab'
- handleBrowserNavigateTab → 'browser.navigateTab'
- handleBrowserFocusTab → 'browser.focusTab'
- handleBrowserGetTabInfo → 'browser.getTabInfo'
- handleBrowserScreenshotTab → 'browser.screenshotTab'
- handleBrowserGetTabContent → 'browser.getTabContent'
- handleBrowserClickElement → 'browser.clickElement'
- handleBrowserTypeText → 'browser.typeText'
- handleBrowserSelectOption → 'browser.selectOption'
- handleBrowserWaitForElement → 'browser.waitForElement'
- handleBrowserQueryElements → 'browser.queryElements'
- handleBrowserGetCookies → 'browser.getCookies'
- handleBrowserSetCookie → 'browser.setCookie'
- handleBrowserDeleteCookies → 'browser.deleteCookies'
- handleBrowserEnableNetworkCapture → 'browser.enableNetworkCapture'
- handleBrowserExecuteScript → 'browser.executeScript'

Also check for any .catch(console.error) in handleBrowserGetNetworkRequests, handleBrowserDisableNetworkCapture, handleBrowserGetConsoleLogs, handleBrowserClearConsoleLogs, and the extension.reload handler.

Use console.warn not console.error — these are expected lifecycle failures.

IMPORTANT: message-router.test.ts has a comment on line 502 that references .catch(console.error) behavior. Update that comment to reflect the new .catch(err => console.warn(...)) pattern.

userStories.title: Add timestamps to MCP server logger
userStories.description: As a developer reading MCP server logs, I want every log line to include an ISO 8601 timestamp so that I can correlate events during hot reloads, WebSocket reconnections, and plugin discovery.
acceptanceCriteria: Every log line output by the MCP server includes an ISO 8601 timestamp between the tag and the message
All four log methods (debug, info, warn, error) include the timestamp
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/mcp-server/src/logger.ts. In each of the four methods (debug, info, warn, error), change the console call to include a timestamp. Example for debug:
  From: console.debug(TAG, ...args)
  To:   console.debug(TAG, new Date().toISOString(), ...args)

Apply the same change to all four methods. The timestamp goes after TAG so the format is:
  [opentabs] 2026-02-18T14:30:00.123Z Config loaded: 2 plugin paths...

This is a minimal change — just add `new Date().toISOString(),` as the second argument to each console.* call.

userStories.title: Standardize error object passing in MCP server log callsites
userStories.description: As a developer debugging the MCP server, I want error objects passed directly to the logger (not err.message) so that stack traces are preserved at debug level and the boilerplate 'err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)' is eliminated.
acceptanceCriteria: No MCP server callsite passes err.message or 'err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)' to log.error or log.warn
All error callsites pass the raw error object: log.error('message:', err) or log.warn('message:', err)
The logger detects Error instances as the last argument and formats them: message on the same line, stack on next line at debug level only
At default info level, error logging shows the error message but not the stack
At OPENTABS_LOG_LEVEL=debug, error logging shows both message and stack

userStories.title: Add debug-level request lifecycle logging for tool dispatch
userStories.description: As a developer debugging tool dispatch failures, I want to see a complete request trace at debug level showing tool resolution, validation, dispatch to extension, and response with duration.
acceptanceCriteria: Setting OPENTABS_LOG_LEVEL=debug produces a trace for every tool dispatch showing: tool name resolution, validation result, dispatch send with request ID, and response with duration in ms
Default info level shows no additional output from these new log lines
All new log lines use log.debug() only
userStories.notes:
Add log.debug() calls at key points in the tool dispatch path.

In platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts, in the tools/call handler:
1. After resolving tool lookup (finding the plugin and tool from the prefixed name):
   log.debug('tool.call:', toolName, '→', pluginName + '/' + toolDef.name)
2. After input validation passes:
   log.debug('tool.call: input validated for', toolName)
3. Before dispatching to extension:
   log.debug('tool.call: dispatching', pluginName + '/' + toolDef.name)
4. After dispatch completes (success or failure), log with duration:
   const startTs = Date.now(); // capture before dispatch
   // ... after dispatch ...
   log.debug('tool.call:', pluginName + '/' + toolDef.name, 'completed in', (Date.now() - startTs) + 'ms')

In platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts:
5. In dispatchToExtension, before sending the WebSocket message:
   log.debug('dispatch → extension:', method, 'id:', id)
6. When a response is received (in the message handler that resolves pending dispatches):
   log.debug('dispatch ← extension: id:', id, 'in', (Date.now() - pending.startTs) + 'ms')
   Note: you may need to add a startTs field to the pending dispatch entry to track timing.

All strictly at debug level. Import log from './logger.js' in both files if not already imported.

userStories.title: Standardize build script log prefixes to [opentabs:build:<target>]
userStories.description: As a developer running build commands, I want build script output to use the platform's [opentabs] prefix convention so that all platform output is filterable with a single prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: All console.log/console.error calls in build-extension.ts use [opentabs:build:<label>] prefix (e.g., [opentabs:build:background])
All console.log/console.error calls in build-side-panel.ts use [opentabs:build:side-panel] prefix
No bare [background], [offscreen], or [side-panel] prefixes exist in build scripts
userStories.notes:
Two files to change:

1. platform/browser-extension/build-extension.ts:
   Change the log prefix from `[${label}]` to `[opentabs:build:${label}]`. There are 4 console calls:
   - Line 40: console.error(`[${label}] Bundle failed:`) → console.error(`[opentabs:build:${label}] Bundle failed:`)
   - Line 52: console.error(`[${label}] Bundle produced no output`) → console.error(`[opentabs:build:${label}] Bundle produced no output`)
   - Line 58: console.log(`[${label}] Bundled successfully`) → console.log(`[opentabs:build:${label}] Bundled successfully`)
   The label variable is either 'background' or 'offscreen'.

2. platform/browser-extension/build-side-panel.ts:
   - Line 23: console.error('Side panel build failed:') → console.error('[opentabs:build:side-panel] Build failed:')
   - Line 30: console.log('[side-panel] Built successfully') → console.log('[opentabs:build:side-panel] Built successfully')
